title: Reproduce the human mitochondrial AT skew diagram in the web app
summary: Build a single-record circular mitochondrial genome diagram with custom circular track slots for GC content, GC skew, AT skew, ticks, labels, and a left legend.
estimatedTime: 5-10 min after the input is available
primaryWorkflow: Circular custom track slots with AT skew
requirements: The human mitochondrial GenBank flat file listed below.
Use a local copy of gbdraw.app or the hosted web app; genome processing runs in the browser.

downloads.label: NC_012920
downloads.note: Download the complete human mitochondrial genome as a GenBank flat file and save it with this filename if you want to match the Gallery session.

quickReproduce.title: Download the Gallery session
quickReproduce.body: Use the Session button in this Gallery entry to save HmmtDNA_ATskew.gbdraw-session.json.

media.alt: Gallery header for the human mitochondrial AT skew diagram with the Session action visible.
media.caption: The Gallery entry keeps the finished interactive SVG, downloadable SVG, and session JSON together.

operations.title: Session button for this Gallery entry

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing session button for this gallery entry.
media.caption: Session button for this Gallery entry in the gallery page view.

quickReproduce.title: Open the web app
quickReproduce.body: Open gbdraw.app, then load the saved .gbdraw-session.json from the session controls.

operations.title: Header session controls
operations.body: Use the rendered web app header toolbar and click Load Session.

media.alt: Actual Circular web app header toolbar with Load Session, Save Session, Reset Settings, undo and redo, the session title, and mode controls visible.
media.caption: The rendered .app-toolbar crop shows the Load Session control in its real header-toolbar context.

quickReproduce.title: Inspect the restored custom stack
quickReproduce.body: The session restores the input GenBank file, circular mode, labels, the left legend, and the explicit circular track stack including the AT skew row.

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing restored custom track stack.
media.caption: Restored custom track stack in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Switch to Circular mode
manualSteps.body: Open gbdraw.app and select Circular mode before adding the mitochondrial GenBank input.

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing mode segmented control.
media.caption: Mode segmented control in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Upload the GenBank file
manualSteps.body: Upload HmmtDNA.gbk. The Gallery session uses the human mitochondrial reference record NC_012920.1.

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing circular genbank uploader.
media.caption: Circular GenBank uploader in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Set the basic circular layout
manualSteps.body: Use the same compact circular defaults as the Gallery figure.
items: Features: CDS, rRNA, tRNA, tmRNA, ncRNA, repeat_region

operations.title: Basic circular layout controls
operations.body: Confirm middle, left legend, labels out.

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing basic circular layout controls.
media.caption: Basic circular layout controls in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Set the dinucleotide calculation window
manualSteps.body: Use a short mitochondrial window so GC content, GC skew, and AT skew all show local structure.

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing dinucleotide window controls.
media.caption: Dinucleotide window controls in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Open Custom Track Slots
manualSteps.body: In the circular layout controls, enable Custom Track Slots and reset to the Middle template if you need a clean starting stack.
items: features: Features, on the axis, lane direction split

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing custom track slots panel.
media.caption: Custom Track Slots panel in the web app session view.

operations.title: Middle template slot stack
operations.body: Confirm features, GC, GC skew slots.

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing middle template slot stack.
media.caption: Middle template slot stack in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Add the AT skew track
manualSteps.body: Add another Dinucleotide skew row inside the axis and use it for AT skew rather than the default GC skew.

media.alt: Generated circular human mitochondrial diagram with GC content, GC skew, and AT skew rings.
media.caption: The finished diagram adds an AT skew ring inside the standard GC content and GC skew rings.

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing add at skew slot.
media.caption: Add AT skew slot in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Add the tick track
manualSteps.body: Keep a Ticks row inside the axis so position labels sit inside the tick marks.

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing tick track slot.
media.caption: Tick track slot in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Prioritize gene labels for CDS features
manualSteps.body: Set qualifier priority for CDS labels to gene so compact labels such as ND1, COX1, ATP6, and CYTB are used instead of long product names.

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing cds label priority controls.
media.caption: CDS label priority controls in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Generate the diagram
manualSteps.body: Click Generate Diagram. The preview should show the labeled mitochondrial feature ring, GC content, GC skew, AT skew, and a left legend that includes AT skew (+) and AT skew (-).

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing generated at skew preview.
media.caption: Generated AT skew preview in the generated preview view.

manualSteps.title: Inspect a feature
manualSteps.body: Click a CDS or RNA feature in the interactive SVG to confirm its gene, product, location, and sequence metadata.

media.alt: Human mitochondrial AT skew diagram with a feature popup open.
media.caption: Feature clicks are useful for checking that label text came from the intended qualifier.

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing feature popup.
media.caption: Feature popup in the popup view.

colorRules.title: This example does not need feature color rules
colorRules.body: The main tutorial point is the custom numeric track stack. Feature colors use the ajisai palette defaults.

colorRules.title: Use gene for compact labels
colorRules.body: For mitochondrial CDS labels, gene is usually more readable than product because product names are long and repetitive.

colorRules.title: Separate feature colors from skew colors
colorRules.body: Feature color rules affect CDS, rRNA, tRNA, and other genome features. GC skew and AT skew colors are set on the Dinucleotide skew slot rows.

colorRules.title: Name custom numeric tracks through legend labels
colorRules.body: Use the slot legend label AT skew so the legend shows AT skew (+) and AT skew (-) instead of another generic skew entry.

postGenerationEdits.title: Click features before changing labels
postGenerationEdits.body: Use the feature popup to inspect gene, product, note, and db_xref values before changing qualifier priority or label overrides.

operations.title: Feature popup
operations.body: Confirm gene, product, location, and sequence metadata.

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing feature popup metadata.
media.caption: Feature popup metadata supports label and qualifier-priority checks.

postGenerationEdits.title: Edit the legend
postGenerationEdits.body: Use the legend editor to adjust captions or order after generation. Keep both AT skew entries visible because the two colors encode positive and negative skew.

media.alt: human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial operation screenshot showing legend editor.
media.caption: Legend editor in the web app session view.

postGenerationEdits.title: Export the figure
postGenerationEdits.body: Download SVG for editable vector output. Use PNG or PDF for quick sharing or draft figures.

operations.title: Export controls
operations.body: Use the rendered Preview export toolbar for SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, or PDF.

media.alt: Actual Circular Preview export toolbar with the DPI selector and SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, and PDF export buttons visible.
media.caption: The rendered .preview-toolbar crop shows DPI plus SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, and PDF export controls.

postGenerationEdits.title: Save a session
postGenerationEdits.body: Save a .gbdraw-session.json after edits so the web app can restore the input, custom track slots, generated output, and SVG adjustments.

operations.title: Session save controls
operations.body: Use the rendered web app header toolbar and click Save Session.

media.alt: Actual Circular web app header toolbar with Save Session, Load Session, Reset Settings, undo and redo, the session title, and mode controls visible.
media.caption: The rendered .app-toolbar crop shows Save Session in the same header toolbar used to load sessions.

troubleshooting.title: AT skew is missing from the legend
troubleshooting.body: Confirm the added slot uses renderer Dinucleotide skew, dinucleotide AT, and legend label AT skew.

troubleshooting.title: The skew rings overlap labels or ticks
troubleshooting.body: Confirm Custom Track Slots are enabled, reset to Middle if needed, and keep GC content, GC skew, AT skew, and ticks inside the axis.

troubleshooting.title: Labels show long product names
troubleshooting.body: Confirm the CDS qualifier priority rule uses gene before regenerating the diagram.

troubleshooting.title: The generated diagram differs from the Gallery image
troubleshooting.body: Confirm input record NC_012920.1, track type middle, labels out, legend left, window 500, step 50, AT skew colors, and the tick label layout.
